event_description: S&P 500 index addition announcement: when S&P Dow Jones Indices announces a stock will join the S&P 500 (after market hours), buy at the next open and hold for 5 trading days. The forced buying by passive index funds drives significant positive abnormal returns.
causal_mechanism: Passive index funds (>$5T in S&P 500 trackers) must buy the newly added stock before the effective date to minimize tracking error. These funds are price-insensitive buyers. Forced demand creates a predictable price pressure. Arbitrageurs front-run index fund buying, amplifying the initial pop. Result: stock rises abnormally from announcement to ~effective date.

event_description: When the FDA issues a clinical/efficacy rejection (CRL, RTF, or AdComm negative vote) for a single-pipeline small biotech, causing a 55%+ single-day crash, short the stock at the open of the crash day and hold for 3 days.
causal_mechanism: After a 55%+ crash from an FDA clinical rejection, institutional holders who were long continue forced selling for 2-5 days as they unwind positions (position limits, redemption pressure, risk management rules). Day-traders who shorted on crash day take profits within 1-2 days but selling pressure from institutional unwinding dominates. The stock has near-zero remaining fundamental value for a single-pipeline drug company post-rejection, so there is no natural buyer base to absorb the continued selling. Market makers widen spreads and reduce size, amplifying the downward drift. This institutional forced-selling channel has been documented in equity event studies (Coval & Stafford 2007 on fire sales).

Related dead end: fda_crl_generic_short — Tested generic 'SHORT small biotech after any CRL' hypothesis. N=8 measured (4 delisted = survivorship bias).
Results: 1d avg=-25.24% (median=-2.84%), 5d avg=-28.51%, positive_rate_1d=50%. Does NOT pass multiple testing (no horizons significant at p<0.05, though p=0.12 at 1d with N=8). 
CRITICAL ISSUES: (1) CMC-only CRLs show POSITIVE 1d returns (AXSM +4.8%, SRRK +4.1%) - wrongly shorting these would lose money. (2) Clinical CRLs have EXTREME variance - OTLK -81% but ITRM +18%, NERV +1.2%. (3) 33% data failure from delistings = survivorship bias. 
DO NOT pursue generic CRL short. The effect is real but too heterogeneous. Need CONDITIONAL sub-hypothesis: (single-asset company) + (clinical deficiency CRL) + (pre-announcement run-up, suggesting market expected approval). That specific sub-case might be consistently very negative, but requires 15+ events with that profile.

event_description: Short single-pipeline small biotech stock at open the trading day after a >55% crash caused by Phase 2/3 clinical trial failure OR FDA Complete Response Letter (CRL) with clinical/efficacy deficiency. Hold 3 days. Qualifying criteria: (1) initial crash >55% on day of announcement, (2) single primary drug/program — company has no other active clinical programs, (3) market cap <$500M at time of event, (4) NOT pre-anticipated — stock not down >20% in prior 10 trading days, (5) clinical/efficacy deficiency (NOT CMC/manufacturing only), (6) company has NO stated regulatory path forward for the failed drug — no announced FDA meeting plans or subgroup-based NDA submissions at time of event (companies with announced path forward bounce instead of continuing lower).
causal_mechanism: When a single-pipeline small biotech's only drug fails Phase 2/3 or receives a clinical CRL, all projected revenue evaporates overnight. The crash is immediate but continuation occurs over 3-5 days because: (1) Institutional portfolio managers require 1-3 days to process and execute forced exits from biotech holdings that violate their 'viable revenue path' mandate; (2) The initial gap-down to market open typically underestimates ultimate fundamental value — the stock's only value was the pipeline drug, so intrinsic value approaches the cash balance, and the market takes 2-3 days to price this in; (3) Retail momentum traders (who see the crash and sell) add downward pressure over days 1-3.

Actual measurement: GOSS Feb 23 2026 Phase 3 PROSERA failure, entry at open Feb 24 (0.388), 1d abnormal=+4.40%, 3d abnormal=+1.55%, 5d abnormal=+9.67%. EXCLUDED on 2 criteria: (1) Criterion 2 FAIL: multi-pipeline company -- has ADX-2191 (Phase 2 active, vitreoretinal lymphoma/RP) and ADX-248/ADX-246 (IND filings planned 2026, metabolic inflammation and dry AMD respectively). event_description: When a large-cap (>\B market cap) CEO is forced out due to documented performance failure (poor financial results sustained over 6+ months, board pressure, earnings misses), SHORT the stock at next-day market open. Hold 1 trading day. Qualifying: CEO departure is abrupt/forced, stock underperformed sector 20%+ in prior 12 months, company had public earnings misses or profit warnings in past year.
causal_mechanism: Board firing a CEO signals problems are worse than management publicly acknowledged. Board has internal visibility management withheld. Institutional investors who were holding on sell on this confirmation signal. Short sellers use announcement as entry. New CEO uncertainty premium applied. Effect is concentrated in Day 1 (p=0.0034); does NOT persist to Day 3+ (p=0.077).

causal_mechanism_criteria.actors_and_incentives: Boards fire CEOs only when situation is unsustainable — bears reputational cost. Institutional holders sell on confirmation of problems they suspected. Short sellers who identified the decline use CEO firing as entry confirmation.
causal_mechanism_criteria.transmission_channel: (1) Bayesian update: board firing reveals hidden severity of problems. (2) Uncertainty premium: new CEO will assess books, possible guidance revision. Effect concentrated at Day 1 open; reverting by Day 3 as uncertainty resolves.
causal_mechanism_criteria.academic_reference: Huson, Parrino & Starks (2001) JFQA: negative stock reactions to forced CEO departures. Warner, Watts & Wruck (1988) JFE: stock underperformance predicts management changes. Fee & Hadlock (2004) JFE: performance-based CEO turnover confirms selection mechanism.

event_description: When 6-9 corporate insiders at the same company buy shares within a short window, the stock outperforms the market over the next 5 trading days. Discovery (2021-2022, n=173): avg 5d abnormal return +3.57%, pos_rate=59.0%, p=0.0001. Validation (2023-2025, n=178): avg 5d abnormal return +7.80%, pos_rate=62.9%, p<0.0001. Both periods pass multiple testing correction.
causal_mechanism: Multiple insiders at the same firm each independently filing Form 4 purchases signals broad internal conviction. At 6-9 insiders, the cluster exceeds random coincidence but remains below the n>=10 level where reporting artifacts and lock-up expirations inflate counts. Market participants take 3-10 days to aggregate Form 4 data across filings, so the signal is slow to be arbitraged. Price drifts upward as the aggregated signal propagates through data vendors and quant fund scanners.

causal_mechanism_criteria.actors_and_incentives: Corporate insiders (officers, directors) risk personal capital when buying shares. A cluster of 6-9 individuals each independently placing buy orders signals high internal confidence — they bear downside risk personally and face SEC scrutiny if trading on MNPI, so frivolous or misleading buying is disincentivized.
causal_mechanism_criteria.transmission_channel: EDGAR Form 4 filings become public within 2 business days of the trade. Data aggregators (OpenInsider, SEC bulk feeds) surface clusters. Informed investors and quant funds scan these feeds, generating buy pressure as the signal propagates. Price drift occurs over 5-10 days as the aggregated signal reaches more market participants.
causal_mechanism_criteria.academic_reference: Seyhun (1998) Investment Intelligence from Insider Trading documents insider buying predicts positive abnormal returns. Cohen, Malloy & Pomorski (2012) Decoding Inside Information (Journal of Finance) show opportunistic insider buys generating 6%+ abnormal returns over 1-6 months.

confounders.market_regime: Backtested across 2021-2022 (bull + rising rate environment) and validated on 2023-2025 (mixed/recovery). Validation abnormal returns are substantially higher (7.8% vs 3.57% at 5d), confirming the effect is not a 2021 bull-market artifact.

event_description: When 6-9 corporate insiders (officers/directors) make open-market stock purchases within 30 days of each other (each >$50K), the stock outperforms the market over the following 3 trading days. This is the optimal cluster size tier: regime-robust (works in both calm and elevated VIX) unlike 3-5 clusters, and signal-clean (unlike 10+ clusters which are noise).
causal_mechanism: Strong insider conviction signal. 6-9 coordinated buyers indicates management expects imminent positive catalyst. Unlike 3-5 insiders (fails in elevated VIX p=0.27), 6-9 insiders overcome market noise even in volatile regimes (p=0.027 in VIX 25-30). Unlike 10+ insiders (coin flip p=0.64 -- contaminated by compensation plans and SPAC mechanics), 6-9 represents genuine coordinated conviction. Entry: long at next open after last cluster filing. Exit: 3 trading days later.

When 6-9 different insiders independently buy, each faces legal risk from trading on material non-public information. 6-9 threshold filters most 10b5-1 pre-planned transactions and compensation plan activity.
causal_mechanism_criteria.transmission_channel: Multiple-insider buying signals positive expectations to the market. Investors monitoring SEC EDGAR Form 4 filings detect the pattern and trade on it, transmitting the signal to prices. Effect peaks at 3-5 days as institutional investors complete position-building.
causal_mechanism_criteria.academic_reference: Kang, Kim & Wang (2018): cluster purchases yield 3.8% abnormal return over 21 days. Lakonishok & Lee (2001): insider purchases strongly predictive. event_description: Insider buying cluster (3+ insiders) where CEO or CFO is one of the buyers. Minimum 3 insiders with open-market Form 4 purchases >=$ 50K each within 30 days. CEO or CFO confirmed via SEC RPTOWNER_TITLE field. Market cap >=$ 500M.
causal_mechanism: CEO and CFO have privileged access to company-wide material information including forward revenue, contract pipeline, cost structure, and strategic plans. Unlike directors who attend quarterly board meetings, CEO/CFO live in the business daily. When they personally commit capital via open-market purchases (not option grants or 10b5-1 plans), they signal undervaluation with the highest-quality information available to any insider. When 3+ insiders cluster-buy AND the CEO/CFO is among them, this is a double confirmation: CEO/CFO superior information plus multi-insider coordination signaling broad agreement. The market takes 3-5 trading days to fully incorporate this signal as information diffuses gradually through Form 4 filing distribution, media coverage, and institutional model updates — consistent with post-earnings announcement drift dynamics.

causal_mechanism_criteria.actors_and_incentives: CEO/CFO are rational economic actors risking personal capital. Open-market code-P purchases require affirmative decision under strict pre-clearance and short-swing profit rules. They have information on the full P&L, not just their committee domain. Purchases are costly commitments unlike sales which have many liquidity motivations.
causal_mechanism_criteria.transmission_channel: Form 4 appears on EDGAR within 2 business days of trade. Financial media and institutional investors parse SEC data and update models. Retail attention increases. Price discovery occurs over 3-5 days, not instantly, because not all participants process the signal simultaneously — creating the abnormal return window.
causal_mechanism_criteria.academic_reference: Cohen, Malloy & Pomorski (2012) 'Decoding Inside Information', Journal of Finance 67(5): CEO/CFO purchases are 12x more predictive than other insider purchases. Separating routine from opportunistic trades and focusing on C-suite officers dramatically improves predictive power.

event_description: When a large-cap company (>$1B market cap) receives its first investment-grade credit rating from any major rating agency (Moody's Baa3, S&P BBB-, or Fitch BBB-), buy at the open of the next trading day and hold for 3 trading days. Qualifying: (1) company has never had IG status from this agency before, or previously lost IG and is now regaining it; (2) upgrade is not accompanied by same-day M&A or earnings release; (3) no other agency already upgraded this company to IG in the preceding 10 trading days. Additional exclusion criterion: stock must not have risen more than 250% in the 24 months prior to the upgrade date (extreme pre-run indicates equity market already priced in credit improvement, neutralizing the signal).
causal_mechanism: A first IG upgrade removes a company from high-yield bond indices and causes buying pressure from IG-mandated investors. IG bond fund managers must purchase new IG issuers to track IG indices (Bloomberg Agg, LQD). The company gains lower-cost debt access, reducing interest burden and improving equity value. Institutional investors use credit ratings as a quality proxy -- an upgrade signals improved balance sheet health. The mechanism is strongest for FIRST IG upgrades: follow-on upgrades show flat-to-negative returns (n=3: avg 5d=-2.06%), confirming the market prices subsequent agency upgrades after the first.

causal_mechanism_criteria.actors_and_incentives: IG-mandated bond fund managers must buy new IG issuers for index compliance. Equity analysts re-rate fundamental quality. Retail investors use credit ratings as quality signal.
causal_mechanism_criteria.transmission_channel: Bond fund inflows reduce yield spreads and lower cost of capital, increasing equity DCF valuation. Stock price benefits through (a) lower discount rate, (b) improved EPS from lower interest expense, (c) positive sentiment from quality signal.
causal_mechanism_criteria.academic_reference: Kliger and Sarig (2000, JF): credit rating upgrades cause significant positive equity returns. Hand, Holthausen and Leftwich (1992, JF): IG vs non-IG threshold crossings have larger equity impact.

backtest_events.note: Negative result: stock up ~400% pre-upgrade (AI/power hype). 3d abnormal=-3.79%. Suggests pre-event run-up >150% may neutralize signal.

OOS test: S&P first-IG upgrade to BBB- (Feb 12 2026, AH). 24m pre-event run-up=220.1% — PASSES <250% filter. 3d abnormal=+1.36% (positive, above 0.5% threshold). 5d=+1.12%, 10d=+6.31%. Direction correct. Counts as validation.

notes: 2026-03-23: VST (Dec 2025 out-of-sample) was negative (-3.79% 3d abnormal). VST had run up ~400% in 12 months prior. Hypothesis: pre-event equity run-up >150% may neutralize first-IG signal (market already priced in credit improvement). Need to add pre-event-run-up filter to hypothesis before next trade.

2026-03-23 (session 2): Pre-event run-up filter analysis completed. CORRECTION to prior note: VST 12m run-up was only 16.7%, NOT ~400%. The 400% figure refers to 24-month window (24m = 402.8%). Key finding: 12m run-up has near-zero correlation with 3d returns (r=-0.045). 24m run-up has moderate negative correlation (r=-0.404, n=13 incl VST). Filter at 150% using 24m window incorrectly excludes FCX (195% 24m, 3d=+6.66%). All 12 discovery events have 24m run-up <200% (max=FCX at 195%). Proposed prospective filter: exclude events where 24m pre-event run-up >250%. This preserves all 12 discovery events while excluding VST-like outliers. Filtered backtest unchanged from original: 3d=2.42%, p=0.021, passes_MT=True.

2026-03-23 (session 3): VRT OOS test — S&P BBB- first-IG upgrade Feb 12 2026 (AH). 24m pre-event run-up = 220.1% (passes <250% filter). Entry at Feb 13 open. 3d abnormal = +1.36% (positive, directionally correct, above 0.5% threshold). 5d = +1.12%, 10d = +6.31%. 1d = -1.62% (noise — consistent with backtest showing 1d is weak). VRT counts as a 2nd OOS positive after VST was excluded by the run-up filter. OOS record now: 1 positive (VRT), 1 negative pre-filter-exclusion (VST, 24m=402%). Signal continues to hold.

2026-03-23 (session 4): Candidate pipeline search. Best near-term candidate: Carnival Corp (CCL). Fitch already upgraded to BBB- (Dec 2025). S&P currently BB+ — upgrade to BBB- estimated 2H 2026 or early 2027. CCL 24m run-up (Mar 2024 - Mar 2026) = 43.8% — EASILY passes <250% filter. CCL is trade-ready when S&P announces BBB- upgrade. All other candidates screened: DAL/LUV/UBER/MSCI/F already IG, NCLH at B+ (too far), CZR too leveraged, MGM BB- (too far). Monitor S&P press releases after CCL quarterly earnings.

2026-03-23 TIMING FILTER INSIGHT (from CCL case): CCL Fitch upgrade (Oct 2, 2025) was announced DURING MARKET HOURS. By Oct 2 close, move was fully priced in (+1.62% abnormal on the day). Next-open entry (-0.99% 3d) was too late — missed the entire move. ALL PRIOR DISCOVERY EVENTS were after-hours announcements. ACTION RULE: Before activating this hypothesis, verify upgrade announcement was after-hours. If announced during market hours → DO NOT TRADE (move already priced in). If announced after-hours → ENTER AT NEXT OPEN as normal. This is not a retroactive exclusion of CCL — it counts as OOS negative (66.7% = 4/6). But it explains the mechanism and improves future trade selection.

Signal is pure noise for large-cap stocks - consistent with efficient market hypothesis. Momentum is already priced in before the 52w high breach. Dead end.
